edge_statistics_sigma_lift_obstruction.underdetermination_statement: The ordered even family is exact once sigma_u and sigma_d are fixed, but current edge statistics do not derive the coefficients selecting sigma_u and sigma_d from S_13, S_23, delta21, rho, and x2.

arbitrary_frame_transport_countermodel.frame_A: Keep the selected public physical frame class fixed at P_star and extend no additional off-canonical labels.
arbitrary_frame_transport_countermodel.frame_B: Attach any nonzero off-canonical frame label f(P)=h(P)L with h(P_star)=0 and with L in a source-unclassified public quark-frame direction.
arbitrary_frame_transport_countermodel.name: selected_class_identity_vs_free_off_canonical_frame_lift
arbitrary_frame_transport_countermodel.why_both_fit_current_corpus: All selected-class artifacts inspect only P_star and the selected public class. The corpus does not emit a classifier that transports or rejects L for arbitrary P.

even_sigma_countermodels.interpretation: Extends the selected-class exact sigma pair off-canonical without using edge statistics. It preserves the canonical point but carries no source-emitted off-canonical response.
even_sigma_countermodels.name: constant_selected_class_extension

even_sigma_countermodels.canonical_preservation_condition: requires an extra normalization/correction because the emitted candidate does not equal the active selected-class sigma pair at the current canonical point
even_sigma_countermodels.interpretation: Uses the live edge-shaped candidate coefficients. It is source-shaped but not theorem-selected, and it fails selected-class exactness without an additional correction law.
even_sigma_countermodels.name: edge_candidate_response_extension

even_sigma_countermodels.interpretation: For any nonzero source-unfixed h and small epsilons, this preserves every selected-class artifact while producing different off-canonical masses. Current artifacts provide no rule that excludes it.
even_sigma_countermodels.name: canonical_vanishing_free_perturbation_family

formal_countermodel_witness.nonidentifiability_result: Because these countermodels agree on every selected-class exact artifact but disagree away from P_star, sigma_u(P), sigma_d(P), kappa(P), and public frame transport are not identifiable from the current source basis.

odd_kappa_countermodels.interpretation: Keeps the odd ray identically silent off-canonical.
odd_kappa_countermodels.name: zero_odd_extension

odd_kappa_countermodels.interpretation: Turns on a nonzero odd payload away from the selected point while preserving the canonical selected-class artifacts. Current artifacts do not emit epsilon or h.
odd_kappa_countermodels.name: canonical_vanishing_odd_response_family

odd_kappa_countermodels.canonical_preservation_condition: only a coordinate representative unless a physical normalization theorem identifies it
odd_kappa_countermodels.interpretation: The current odd artifact records this as a unit representative, not a value law.
odd_kappa_countermodels.name: unit_representative_extension

lane_closure_verdict.repo_runtime_browser_contract_status: shared candidate/evidence surface only; theorem-grade off-canonical promotion disallowed
lane_closure_verdict.why_this_is_final_for_current_corpus: The attached source artifacts admit distinct canonical-preserving off-canonical completions for both the even sigma pair and the odd kappa payload. A direct arbitrary-P evaluator would therefore choose extra data not emitted by the present theorem corpus.

notes: The selected public exact quark theorem is closed and can be used as a theorem-grade static evaluator.
The selected-public-class pure-B source payload is now data-bearing when present.
Those selected-class closures do not supply an arbitrary-P off-canonical sigma or odd-scale family.

odd_response_scale_obstruction.underdetermination_statement: The current odd response closes a signed projector direction but leaves a one-scalar kappa ray. Different admissible kappa values change the odd payload while satisfying the present structural guards.
